Association football club in Gibraltar

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c1/Victoria_Stadium-west_stands.JPG/250px-Victoria_Stadium-west_stands.JPG)](/wiki/File:Victoria_Stadium-west_stands.JPG)

United play their home games in the [Victoria Stadium](/wiki/Victoria_Stadium_(Gibraltar) "Victoria Stadium (Gibraltar)")

**Manchester 62 F.C.**[[6]](#cite_note-6) is a [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") club based in [Gibraltar](/wiki/Gibraltar "Gibraltar"). The club currently competes in the [Gibraltar Football League](/wiki/Gibraltar_Football_League "Gibraltar Football League").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Manchester_62_F.C.&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The club was founded as *Manchester United F.C.* in 1962,[[7]](#cite_note-7) named in honour of the [English](/wiki/England "England") club Manchester United by a group of supporters, after the manager [Matt Busby](/wiki/Matt_Busby "Matt Busby") gave them permission to use the name.[[8]](#cite_note-8) They play in the [Gibraltar Football League](/wiki/Gibraltar_Football_League "Gibraltar Football League")'s First Division.[[9]](#cite_note-9) United play their home games in the [Victoria Stadium](/wiki/Victoria_Stadium_(Gibraltar) "Victoria Stadium (Gibraltar)").[[10]](#cite_note-10) In 2013, [UEFA](/wiki/UEFA "UEFA") gave the Gibraltar Football League one place each in the qualifying rounds of both the [Europa League](/wiki/Europa_League "Europa League") and the [Champions League](/wiki/UEFA_Champions_League "UEFA Champions League"), meaning that, for the first time, the two clubs could potentially meet in the same competition.[[11]](#cite_note-gib_cl_spot-11)

In June 2022, after years playing as a members-owned amateur club, Manchester 62 was sold to [Pittsburgh City United](/wiki/Pittsburgh_City_United_FC "Pittsburgh City United FC") founder Michael Anton Monsour.[[12]](#cite_note-12) As part of the takeover, the club announced plans to follow the lead of Pittsburgh City United by mandating protective headgear for its players, subject to approval from the [Gibraltar Football Association](/wiki/Gibraltar_Football_Association "Gibraltar Football Association").[[13]](#cite_note-13)

As part of the takeover, the club filmed a documentary and announced plans in March 2023 to submit a report to [UEFA](/wiki/UEFA "UEFA") regarding the impact of protective headgear on head injuries.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

On 19 February 2023, the club announced that 21-year-old [YouTuber](/wiki/YouTuber "YouTuber") Theo Ogden would assume the role of technical director, making Ogden the youngest person to take up the role at a professional European club, however, Ogden has since left the role.[[15]](#cite_note-15)[[16]](#cite_note-16) This was followed by the appointment of [Anthony Limbrick](/wiki/Anthony_Limbrick "Anthony Limbrick") as manager in June 2023.[[17]](#cite_note-17) However, the club suffered huge setbacks with investors pulling funding mid-season, leaving the club in a precarious position with multiple players leaving after the club failed to pay them. The club's future was secured in May 2024 with a $20 million cash injection from [angel investor](/wiki/Angel_investor "Angel investor") Takashi Cheng, through his private equity firm Monstranamus.[[18]](#cite_note-18)

Manchester 62 FC fans are known for their unique tradition of singing "Gibraltar, Rock and Roll," a modified version of Chuck Berry's "Rock and Roll Music," during home games. The song has become the unofficial anthem of the club, and it is played before every match at Victoria Stadium.

## Club names

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Manchester_62_F.C.&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Club names")]

* 1962–2000 : Manchester United (Gibraltar) Football Club.
* 2000–2002 : Manchester United Eurobet Football Club.[[19]](#cite_note-19)
* 2002–2008 : Manchester United (Gibraltar) Football Club.
* 2009–2013 : Manchester United Digibet Football Club.[[20]](#cite_note-20)
* 2013– : Manchester 62 Football Club.

## Sponsors

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Manchester_62_F.C.&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Sponsors")]

From 2013 to 2022, the club was sponsored by [CEPSA](/wiki/CEPSA "CEPSA") Gibraltar. Upon the takeover by Monsour, the club partnered with Gibraltar Alzheimer's and Dementia Society (GADS).[[21]](#cite_note-21) As of 2024, the club's kits are supplied by [Joma](/wiki/Joma "Joma"). In 2023, the club partnered with Unequal Technologies to provide protective headgear for players.[[22]](#cite_note-22) The club's shirt sponsors since 2023 are the [Concussion Legacy Foundation](/wiki/Concussion_Legacy_Foundation "Concussion Legacy Foundation").

## Honours

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Manchester_62_F.C.&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Honours")]

**[Gibraltar Premier Division](/wiki/Gibraltar_Premier_Division "Gibraltar Premier Division"): (7)**

* Champions: 1974–75, 1976–77, 1978–79, 1979–80, 1983–84, 1994–95, 1998–99

**[Gibraltar Second Division](/wiki/Gibraltar_Second_Division "Gibraltar Second Division"): (1)**

* Champions: 1973–74

**[Rock Cup](/wiki/Rock_Cup "Rock Cup"): (3)**

* Winners: 1976–77, 1979–80, 2002–03

**[Gibraltar Premier Cup](/wiki/Gibraltar_Premier_Cup "Gibraltar Premier Cup"): (7)**

* Winners: 1975–76, 1976–77, 1980–81, 1984–85, 1985–86, 1994–95, 1998–99

**[Pepe Reyes Cup](/wiki/Pepe_Reyes_Cup "Pepe Reyes Cup"): (2)**

* Winners: 2003, 2006

GFA Challenge Trophy

* Winners: [2021–22](/wiki/2021%E2%80%9322_Gibraltar_National_League "2021–22 Gibraltar National League")
